_publications._publicationTitle: Modulation of Ras signaling alters the toxicity of hydroquinone, a benzene metabolite and component of cigarette smoke.

_contextSnippet: Nf1−/− progenitors demonstrated significantly increased survival to HQ exposure in comparison to WT (Figure 3) at 10 μM [p-value 0.0010]
resourceName: Mx1-Cre, Nf1 flox/flox mice (129SV × C57BL/6 J)
details: Nf1−/− hematopoietic progenitors demonstrated significantly increased survival to hydroquinone exposure compared to WT at 10 μM (p-value 0.0010)
